exampleSentences: Anh không thể chịu đựng tiếng ồn lớn suốt cả đêm.
Chị ấy phải chịu đựng thời tiết nóng khi làm việc ngoài trời.
Em đã chịu đựng nhiều khó khăn để đạt được mục tiêu.

definitions.es: Tolerar o soportar algo difícil o desagradable.
definitions.en: To tolerate or bear something difficult or unpleasant.
definitions.vi: Chịu đựng một điều gì đó khó khăn hoặc khó chịu.

example.en: I have to endure this pain.
example.vi: Tôi phải chịu đựng cơn đau này.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He cannot endure loud noise all night.
She has to endure hot weather when working outside.
I have endured many difficulties to achieve my goal.
notes:
chịu đựng = to endure, to put up with something difficult, painful, or annoying for a period of time. It implies you don’t like it, but you keep going anyway.

It’s a verb, usually followed by something negative: pain, pressure, stress, loss, criticism, heat, noise. It often talks about emotional or physical suffering.

Close synonym: nhẫn nhịn (more about holding back feelings). Very common shorter form: chịu nổi / không chịu nổi (can/can’t stand).

translation.en: I have to endure this pain.

notes_md:
**chịu đựng** = to endure, to put up with something difficult, painful, or annoying for a period of time. It implies you don’t like it, but you keep going anyway.

It’s a verb, usually followed by something negative: pain, pressure, stress, loss, criticism, heat, noise. It often talks about emotional or physical suffering.


Close synonym: **nhẫn nhịn** (more about holding back feelings). Very common shorter form: **chịu nổi / không chịu nổi** (can/can’t stand).

content.sentence: Anh ấy có thể ___ mọi khó khăn.
